From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

The **Catholic Diocese of Lindisfarne** is a [titular see](/wiki/Titular_see "Titular see") of the [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Roman_Catholic_Bishop_of_Lindisfarne&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The diocese existed as a regular see from the seventh century until Bishop [Aldhun](/wiki/Aldhun "Aldhun") moved it to Durham in 995.

In 1970, the [Pope Paul VI](/wiki/Pope_Paul_VI "Pope Paul VI") revived it as the name of a [titular see](/wiki/Titular_see "Titular see") ([Latin](/wiki/Latin "Latin"): *Lindisfarna*). The first three officeholders served as [auxiliary bishops](/wiki/Auxiliary_bishop "Auxiliary bishop") in the [Diocese of Westminster](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Westminster "Roman Catholic Diocese of Westminster"). The fourth is an [apostolic nuncio](/wiki/Nuncio "Nuncio") and was granted the personal title of archbishop. As of 2024[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Roman_Catholic_Bishop_of_Lindisfarne&action=edit), all titular Bishops of Lindisfarne have been British or Irish.

## Holders of the title

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Roman_Catholic_Bishop_of_Lindisfarne&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Holders of the title")]

* [Victor Guazzelli](/wiki/Victor_Guazzelli "Victor Guazzelli") (1970[[1]](#cite_note-1)–2004)
* [John Arnold](/wiki/John_Arnold_(bishop) "John Arnold (bishop)") (2005–2014), later [Bishop of Salford](/wiki/Bishop_of_Salford "Bishop of Salford")
* [John Wilson](/wiki/John_Wilson_(bishop) "John Wilson (bishop)") (2016–2019),[[2]](#cite_note-2) later [Archbishop of Southwark](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Southwark "Archbishop of Southwark")
* [Michael Francis Crotty](/wiki/Michael_Francis_Crotty "Michael Francis Crotty"), with the dignity of archbishop (2020[[3]](#cite_note-3)–present)
